title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Legacy FC debuted in NWSL with over 30,000 fans at Gillette Stadium, highlighting a Boston-New York rivalry.
Gotham FC won 1-0 against Boston Legacy FC, with Esther González scoring the only goal of the match.
The match saw an NWSL record-tying eight yellow cards, including a red for Boston's Bianca St-Georges.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13 to kick off St. Patrick's Day celebrations.
The band showcased new and old songs, including politically charged tracks like 'Citizen I.C.E.' reflecting their stance on immigration.
Opening acts included Boston punk bands The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown, setting the tone for the night.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, will run the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel to support Joslin Diabetes Center.
Diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at 11, Bennett found hope and guidance from Joslin Diabetes Center.
Bennett aims to raise awareness and support for people with Type 1 Diabetes through his marathon participation.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter, from Springfield, received her fifth Oscar nomination, making her the most-nominated Black woman in Oscars history.
Carter made history in 2019 as the first Black person to win the Best Costume Design Oscar and won a second in 2023.
Her latest nomination for 'Sinners' coincides with the film receiving a record 16 Oscar nominations.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if a proposed property-tax override is not approved by voters.
The school district needs $18.5 million over three years to maintain current staffing and avoid service cuts.
The override will be decided by Brookline voters at the annual town election on May 5.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act aims to ban the sale of dogs and cats in pet shops in Massachusetts.
The bill encourages partnerships between pet shops and animal shelters for adoptions to reduce puppy mill sources.
Legislation also seeks to address housing and insurance barriers to increase accessibility to pet ownership.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts officials urge water conservation as drought intensifies, despite heavy February snowfall.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts are at Level 3 Critical Drought, while other areas face milder drought levels.
State recommends water-saving measures; Massachusetts Water Resources Authority system not currently in drought.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph explores why straight women are obscuring boyfriends in social media photos to challenge traditional relationship norms.
Joseph's essay suggests women may not want to center their identities around male partners, sparking widespread online debate.
The trend reflects broader questions about modern relationships and challenges the norm of prioritizing romantic partnerships.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O’Neill is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
O’Neill's fiancée's sister, Reagan Carey, was a camper at the camp while battling rare bone cancer.
Reagan Carey, now in remission, serves as a camp counselor at the same camp that supported her.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Philadelphia Eagles are not trading wide receiver A.J. Brown to the New England Patriots at this time.
Trade discussions may resume closer to June 1 when salary cap relief could make a deal more feasible for the Eagles.
The Patriots previously offered a first- and third-round pick for Brown, but Philadelphia reportedly sought higher compensation.
